description: You are a satellite data technician at NOAA's Pacific Tsunami Warning Center configuring GPredict for polar-orbiting weather satellite reception across three Pacific ground stations. Configure the following ground stations: Ewa_Beach (21.3069 N, 158.0872 W, 12m), Tiyan (13.4834 N, 144.7977 E, 77m), and Pago_Pago (14.2781 S, 170.7025 W, 3m). Create a tracking module named 'PacificWX' containing NOAA 15 (NORAD 25338), NOAA 18 (NORAD 28654), NOAA 19 (NORAD 33591), SUOMI NPP (NORAD 37849), and FENGYUN 3B (NORAD 37214). Set Ewa_Beach as the default ground station and configure the application to use metric units (kilometers and km/s). Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm setting up a new GPredict installation for the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center to track polar weather satellites. We need three ground stations configured: Ewa_Beach (our main facility in Hawaii) at 21.3069 N, 158.0872 W, 12m alt. Then Tiyan in Guam at 13.4834 N, 144.7977 E, 77m alt. And finally Pago_Pago in American Samoa at 14.2781 S (Southern Hemisphere!), 170.7025 W, 3m alt. Next, please create a new module named PacificWX and add these weather satellites to it: NOAA 15 (25338), NOAA 18 (28654), NOAA 19 (33591), SUOMI NPP (37849), and FENGYUN 3B (37214). After that, go into preferences and make Ewa_Beach the default ground station, and also switch the global units to metric.
